title: install.ps1 (Windows one-line installer from source)

context.goal: Mirror install.sh for Windows. Two execution modes: (a) source build like Linux script, (b) optional download-prebuilt-from-release if available. Default = source build until T11 release pipeline lands; then T12 will switch default to release download.

prereq_install_via_winget.purpose: MSVC C++ compiler (must include workload Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.VCTools)

steps: Detect winget: if (-not (Get-Command winget -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { Write-Error 'winget required (Windows 10 1809+ with App Installer)'; exit 1 }

For each prereq id: if not already installed, run `winget install --id <id> -e --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements`. For VS Build Tools also pass `--override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.VCTools --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"`
Run `rustup default stable` if no default toolchain set
Clone or update repo at $INSTALL_DIR (mirror install.sh logic: if .git exists, fetch+reset; else git clone --depth 1 --branch $BRANCH)

Locate produced installer: Get-ChildItem -Path src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/*.exe | Select-Object -First 1 ; Start-Process the installer with -Wait OR print path and instruct user to double-click

execution_policy_note.problem: User may have Restricted execution policy.
execution_policy_note.guidance_in_README: Recommend running as: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "iwr -useb https://.../install.ps1 | iex"

acceptance: Fresh Windows 11 VM with only winget: full script completes without manual intervention (modulo VS Build Tools dialog)
Resulting NSIS installer launches and installs Dictate to per-user location
After install, dictate.exe appears in Start Menu and runs
Script idempotent: re-running detects existing tools, skips re-install
